title: Maven Local-Repository Provenance Manifest
description: A provenance manifest: a list of artifacts written to the Maven local repository during a build, as produced by the provenance extension (see design.md §6). The extension writes two files with this IDENTICAL shape — they are distinguished by file name, not by content: the project manifest (e.g. repo-provenance.json) lists the PROJECT-determined set, the implicit manifest (e.g. repo-provenance-implicit.json) lists the IMPLICIT (Maven-determined) set. Each is one aggregated, deduplicated file per build (including reactor builds). The artifacts array MUST be canonical: sorted by (groupId, artifactId, version, type, classifier), each entry's files sorted, UTF-8/LF, no timestamps or run-specific data. The PROJECT manifest MUST additionally be byte-identical across Maven versions for the same project sources (design §2/§9.2); the IMPLICIT manifest is Maven-version-specific by nature and is not.

schemaVersion.description: Manifest schema version. Bump on any breaking change to this schema.

artifacts.description: The classified set of artifacts (PROJECT or IMPLICIT depending on which file this is). Sorted. May be empty.

version.description: The resolved version actually present in the local repository.

type.description: Artifact type / packaging (e.g. jar, pom, war). For a coordinate present only as a POM (e.g. a parent POM), use "pom".

classifier.description: Optional artifact classifier (e.g. sources, javadoc). Omit when absent.

files.description: Repository-relative POSIX paths of every file belonging to this artifact (primary file, .pom, checksum sidecars). Sorted. No leading slash; forward slashes only.
